era_context: Akkadian Empire under Naram-Sin; Old Kingdom Egypt (6th Dynasty) in decline; Harappan civilization in mature phase; Xia Dynasty traditionally dated to this era. The 4.2 kya climate event is ongoing, with significant paleoclimatic impacts across Eurasia and Africa.

events.title: Akkadian Empire under Naram-Sin; continued military campaigns
events.description: Naram-Sin's reign continues with ongoing military campaigns to maintain Akkadian control over Mesopotamia and extend influence into surrounding regions. The empire's military apparatus is stretched by the need to suppress rebellions and defend against external pressure. Akkadian administrative texts and inscriptions from this period document the empire's continued functioning, but also hint at increasing strain.
events.certainty_note: Naram-Sin's reign is well-attested, but precise year-by-year chronology is uncertain. The year -2203 is assigned by convention.

events.title: Egyptian 6th Dynasty; continued decline and regional fragmentation
events.description: The 6th Dynasty of Egypt continues its decline. Pepi II's reign is marked by weakening central authority and increasing provincial autonomy. The Nile's inundation remains erratic, exacerbating economic stress. Regional governors accumulate power and resources. The state's ability to organize large-scale projects (such as pyramid construction) has effectively ceased.

sources.name: Egyptian king lists and tomb inscriptions
Paleoclimatic records indicating reduced Nile inundation

events.certainty_note: The 6th Dynasty's decline is well-documented, but precise dating within the dynasty remains uncertain.

events.title: Harappan civilization in mature phase; trade networks active
events.description: The Indus Valley Civilization continues in its mature phase. Urban centers remain thriving. Trade networks continue to connect the Indus Valley to Mesopotamia and the Persian Gulf. Standardized weights and measures indicate sophisticated economic administration. No major disruptions are evident at this time, though environmental stress may be beginning to accumulate.

sources.name: Archaeological evidence from Indus Valley sites

events.certainty_note: The Harappan civilization's continued flourishing is well-attested archaeologically.
cross_references: Part of broader Harappan mature phase

disconfirming_evidence: No specific disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: The year -2203 is not distinctly documented in historical sources. Most events of this period are dated to broader ranges. The surviving sources are heavily weighted toward Mesopotamia and Egypt. Other regions are underrepresented or absent from the written record.

graph_edges.note: Naram-Sin's reign continues toward the Akkadian collapse
